problem text: Consider $$ B_t $$ as a standard Brownian Motion. Determine the variance of $$ X_t = \displaystyle \int_0^t s \, dB_s $$ expressed as a function of $$ t $$. The solution will take the form $$ kt^3 $$ for some constant $$ t $$. Identify $$ k $$.
problem solution: Here, the integrand is a deterministic function $f(s)$, making the integral have a normal distribution with a mean of $0$ and a variance given by $$ \displaystyle \int_0^t (f(s))^2 ds $$. In this scenario, we have $f(s) = s$, thus the variance becomes $$ \displaystyle \int_0^t s^2 ds = \dfrac{t^3}{3} $$. This results in $$ k = \dfrac{1}{3} $$.
problem hint: Notice that the integrand is a deterministic function $f(s)$, leading to the integral being normally distributed with a mean of $0$ and a variance described by $$ \displaystyle \int_0^t (f(s))^2 \, ds $$.
